blocks.content:
Handling and notifications determine what your alert does at the edges and who hears about it. No-data and error handling decide what happens when a query returns nothing or fails — without it, a broken exporter can either fire false alarms or hide a real outage. Notifications make sure the alert reaches the right people through the right channel.

An alert that fires but reaches no one is no better than no alert at all.

Saving the rule is the moment it becomes active and Grafana begins evaluating it on your schedule.

In the next milestone, you learn how to configure handling, select a contact point, add an annotation, and save.
